brief: Episode packaged into a publish-ready summary with three clip moments: why tiny binaries outperform bloated stacks, how artifact contracts reduce handoff friction, and why GitHub can act as the control plane for lightweight products. Show notes and repurposing copy are ready for release.
compositionNote: This one episode fans out into multiple deliverables with tiny single-purpose binaries. You can stop at clips, keep going to packaging, or swap in bonfyre-narrate and bonfyre-distribute without changing the rest of the stack.

moduleNotes.bonfyre-transcribe: bonfyre-transcribe turns the source episode into the base transcript artifact that every later stage reuses.
moduleNotes.bonfyre-transcript-clean: bonfyre-transcript-clean normalizes the raw transcript so later steps do not need to solve cleanup again.
moduleNotes.bonfyre-paragraph: bonfyre-paragraph restructures the transcript into readable blocks for downstream summarization and rendering.
moduleNotes.bonfyre-brief: bonfyre-brief creates the summary object that can become notes, article copy, or narration.
moduleNotes.bonfyre-clips: bonfyre-clips identifies reusable moments without changing the rest of the episode artifact flow.
moduleNotes.bonfyre-render: bonfyre-render turns the same structured episode artifacts into human-friendly outputs.
moduleNotes.bonfyre-pack: bonfyre-pack collects the deliverables into one publish-ready bundle.
moduleNotes.bonfyre-emit: bonfyre-emit pushes the packaged result into release surfaces like feeds and published payloads.

outputNotes.show notes: bonfyre-brief and bonfyre-render generate a publishable summary layer from the cleaned transcript. This is the fastest lightweight output for an episode.
outputNotes.clip list: bonfyre-clips marks reusable moments from the same episode artifact, so short-form outputs stay linked to the full transcript and summary.
outputNotes.article draft: The article draft is the repurposing surface: the same transcript can become prose without rebuilding the pipeline around a separate content system.
outputNotes.RSS-ready bundle: bonfyre-pack and bonfyre-emit package the episode for actual distribution. This is where the app stops being a summary demo and becomes a small publishing system.

compare.title: With And Without The New Layer
compare.note: The floor already turns one episode into a usable summary surface. The orchestration layer earns its place only when it fans that same artifact into more publish-ready outputs without changing the source object.

floor.title: Episode summary and structure only
floor.copy: This is the always-on path: transcript cleanup, paragraph structure, and the brief. It gets you the episode object and readable notes without the extra distribution fanout.

metrics.You get: Show notes from one episode source
metrics.Good when: You want the core episode summary first

layered.title: Same episode, full repurposing fanout
layered.copy: When the policy layer clears its gates, the same episode artifact branches into clips, article copy, and a feed-ready bundle. Bonfyre stays composable because the source artifact never changes.

metrics.Adds: Clips, article copy, and feed packaging
metrics.Why it matters: One episode becomes several publish-ready assets
